matchups.tacticalAdvantage: Duke enters as the overall No. 1 seed after a 32-2 season. Despite late-season injuries to Caleb Foster, the talent gap anchored by Cameron Boozer is too wide for Siena to overcome. Duke fits the optimal AI Intellipick profile with top-tier efficiency balance (No. 1 in KenPom) and carries a massive -27.5 betting spread to advance.

Ohio State relies heavily on senior guard Bruce Thornton (124.3 AdjO), but TCU brings an elite defense (97.8 AdjD) and enters having won nine of their last 11 games. Ohio State is currently favored by a narrow -2.5 points, reflecting TCU's ability to drag efficient offenses into a grind.

Northern Iowa is utterly atrocious on the offensive glass, ranking 356th nationally. St. John's ranks 28th nationally in offensive rebounds and will overpower Northern Iowa with sheer athleticism. St. John's is favored by -9.5 points, supported by their No. 17 KenPom rank compared to Northern Iowa's No. 72 rating.

Kansas features potential top NBA pick Darryn Peterson, though minutes limits have been a concern. CBU is making its first NCAA tournament appearance in program history and relies on the fiery scoring of Dominique Daniels. Kansas is listed as a solid -12.5 favorite, boasting a No. 21 KenPom ranking.

Louisville is missing primary playmaker Mikel Brown Jr. due to a back injury. South Florida plays at a blistering pace and ranks second nationally in rebounds (43 per game). Louisville is favored by -7.5 but qualifies only as a fringe AI Intellipick, making the No. 47 KenPom ranked Bulls a highly viable upset.

Michigan State extended its Big Ten record to 28 consecutive NCAA Tournament appearances. They share the ball exceptionally well and face a Summit League champion in NDSU. The Spartans are -15.5 favorites and fit the AI Intellipick criteria with excellent top-tier efficiency balance.

UCF features an explosive offense averaging 81 points per game and plays a tough style. UCLA relies heavily on the production of Tyler Bilodeau and Donovan Dent but has battled uneven performances. UCLA is favored by -6.5, but UCF's No. 54 KenPom rating and explosiveness make them a dangerous opponent.

Furman secured a No. 15 seed via the Southern Conference automatic bid and is a dangerous mid-major, but UConn under Dan Hurley is a dynastic mastermind with too much depth. UConn fits a fringe AI Intellipick profile due to a slightly slower pace, but oddsmakers heavily back them as -18.5 favorites.

Arizona opened the season with 23 straight wins and is the model's projected national champion, featuring top-five ranks in both offensive and defensive efficiency. Arizona fits the ideal AI Intellipick model, resulting in a staggering -29.5 point spread.

Villanova's offense is dangerously reliant on three-point variance, and they are missing rotation piece Matt Hodge. Utah State possesses a highly balanced offensive attack. Oddsmakers agree with this upset, installing No. 30 KenPom Utah State as a -1.5 point favorite over No. 33 KenPom Villanova.

High Point enters on a fourteen-game winning streak and thrives on offensive execution. Wisconsin ranks 177th nationally in opponent effective field goal percentage. Wisconsin is favored by -12.5, but No. 92 KenPom High Point's momentum creates a statistical upset opportunity.

Arkansas features standout freshman scorer Darius Acuff Jr. and won the SEC Tournament, but sharp money has heavily favored Hawai'i to cover the +15.5 spread. Arkansas ranks No. 15 in KenPom, but defensive vulnerabilities make the Rainbow Warriors an attractive target.

Texas advanced to the Round of 64 after a gritty win over NC State in the First Four, carrying momentum into a clash against BYU's acclaimed freshman AJ Dybantsa. Texas holds the momentum, while No. 23 KenPom BYU attempts to overcome the loss of key rotation piece Richie Saunders.

Gonzaga secured a No. 3 seed but is considered less dominant than classic Bulldogs teams. They face Conference USA representative Kennesaw State. Gonzaga remains an elite AI Intellipick with top efficiency ratings, making them massive -18.5 point favorites.

Missouri dropped to the 10-line late in the season after struggling down the stretch, drawing a tough, battle-tested ACC opponent in Miami. Miami is favored by -2.5 and holds the edge in analytics with a No. 31 KenPom rating compared to Missouri's No. 52.
Purdue enters at 27-8 after winning the Big Ten Tournament, facing tournament first-timer and Atlantic Sun champion Queens. Purdue's No. 8 KenPom rating makes them overwhelming -23.5 point favorites.

Howard advanced out of the First Four, but Michigan boasts the country's most efficient defense (No. 1 AdjD), anchored by 7-foot-7 rim protector Aday Mara. Michigan fits the perfect AI Intellipick profile, ensuring an immense talent mismatch.

Georgia leads the nation in fast-break points (19.9 per game). Sharp money heavily backs Georgia due to Saint Louis ranking 230th in offensive turnovers. Georgia is favored by -1.5 points, driven by extreme tempo numbers.

Texas Tech lost star forward JT Toppin to a torn ACL, forcing them into a predictable, perimeter-reliant offense. Akron ranks in the top fifteen nationally in both two-point and three-point percentage. Texas Tech is favored by -8.5, but No. 64 KenPom Akron makes this a prime upset target.

Alabama lost guard Aden Holloway to an indefinite suspension, severely compromising their perimeter spacing. Hofstra plays at a glacial pace (334th nationally) and utilizes a massive frontcourt to exploit Alabama's 292nd-ranked defensive rebounding. Alabama is a -10.5 favorite, but stylistic mismatches favor Hofstra.

Miami (OH) built a 31-1 record on an artificially inflated, weak schedule. Tennessee is battle-tested, healthy, and will overwhelm Miami with SEC-tier speed and length. Tennessee ranks No. 16 in KenPom, providing a stark reality check for the MAC champion.

Virginia secured a No. 3 seed with their trademark pack-line defense and will look to suffocate the Horizon League champion. Virginia qualifies as a fringe AI Intellipick with elite efficiency numbers, driving their status as -17.5 point favorites.

Kentucky struggles to score in half-court sets and is missing point guard Jaland Lowe (shoulder injury). Santa Clara is well-positioned structurally. Kentucky is narrowly favored by -2.5 points, leaving room for No. 35 KenPom Santa Clara to strike.

Iowa State relies on the interior dominance of Audi Crooks and boasts incredible defensive metrics. Tennessee State represents the Ohio Valley Conference auto-bid. Iowa State matches the exact AI Intellipick criteria with elite defense, commanding a massive -23.5 spread.

Florida is the defending national champion and won the SEC regular-season title. Prairie View advanced out of the First Four, but the talent gap is massive. Florida is an elite No. 4 in KenPom and a fringe AI Intellipick, assuring a blowout victory.

Expected to be a severe clash of slow-paced styles. The betting total opened at a first-round low of 129 points. Iowa brings strong March experience. Iowa is a slight -1.5 favorite, heavily influenced by Clemson missing key rebounder Carter Welling.

Vanderbilt is severely underseeded, ranking 12th nationally in KenPom. Vanderbilt shoots 79% from the free-throw line and features physical forwards equipped to exploit McNeese's poor defensive rebounding. Vanderbilt is a -10.5 favorite and qualifies as a fringe AI Intellipick.

Nebraska heavily regressed late in the season and struggles against elite interior physicality. Troy consistently limits shot volume and efficiency at the basket through elite rim protection. Nebraska is favored by -13.5, but their late-season metrics expose vulnerabilities.

UNC lost interior enforcer Caleb Wilson to a season-ending hand fracture. VCU's relentless ball pressure is designed to disrupt half-court sets, exploiting UNC's compromised frontcourt. UNC is only favored by -1.5 points as No. 45 KenPom VCU holds a high statistical probability for the upset.

Illinois possesses the country's most productive attack. Penn arrives hot off an overtime thriller to win the Ivy League tournament under coach Fran McCaffery, but faces a massive talent deficit. Illinois sits at No. 7 in KenPom, justifying their enormous -20.5 point spread.

A heavy stylistic clash: Saint Mary's dictates a slow, defensive slog and leads the nation with an 81% free-throw rate, while Texas A&M pushes a fast track meet. Saint Mary's is a -2.5 favorite and a fringe AI Intellipick.

Houston was placed in the South Region, setting up a highly motivated run toward a potential regional final in their hometown at the Toyota Center. Houston ranks No. 5 in KenPom and supports a -19.5 point spread.
